prompt:
Take the FIRST provided reference image (the v1 step 21 dual-state illustration with LEFT: hand lowering blood-filled vial into white protector tube body, ARROW, RIGHT: hand placing black DIALED cap onto the assembled tube) and reproduce it PIXEL-FAITHFULLY in EVERY element — same dual-state composition, same hand poses, same vial appearance (transparent tube + red blood + plain ribbed cap), same arrow, same line-art style on pure white background, same line weight, same outlined embossed letter style, same HEALTH text on the body (first H at top, last H at bottom, book-spine sideways letters reading top-down forward), same DIALED text on the cap (first D at top, last D at bottom, book-spine sideways letters reading top-down forward, matching the same letter rotation direction as HEALTH).

>>> THE ONLY FIX <<< — the BLACK CAP and the WHITE BODY currently have MISMATCHED DIAMETERS. In the v1 reference, the cap appears slightly NARROWER than the body, with a visible step / shoulder where they meet. This is WRONG.

The correct geometry (matching the SECOND provided reference image — the approved standalone tube anchor): the BLACK CAP and the WHITE BODY share the EXACT SAME DIAMETER. They are flush against each other — the cap's outer edge sits perfectly continuous with the body's outer edge, with only a thin horizontal seam line indicating where they meet. NO step, NO shoulder, NO narrowing at the cap/body junction. The assembled tube looks like one continuous cylinder of uniform diameter from top to bottom, just divided into a short black cap section on top and a slightly taller white body section below.

Widen the cap so its diameter matches the body's diameter exactly. Both sections should be the same width — a single continuous cylinder split into two color zones (black cap on top, white body on bottom) at a clean horizontal seam.

Apply this fix in BOTH the LEFT state's body (the body has no cap yet, but its diameter should remain consistent and match what the cap will be) and the RIGHT state's assembled tube (cap and body diameter match exactly).

Do NOT modify anything else — same dual-state composition, same hand poses, same vial appearance, same blood, same ribbed vial cap, same arrow, same line weight, same white background, same line-art aesthetic, same correctly-oriented embossed text on both sections. The ONLY change is making the black cap's diameter equal to the white body's diameter so the assembled tube is one continuous cylinder of uniform width.

No frame, no border, no other labels, no numbers.
